name: Quentin Samudio - English
tagline: Research engineer specialised in power system modelling and energy transition analysis. Experience in large-scale modelling, urban energy systems, and collective self-consumption. Strong Python background and contributor to applied research projects on low-carbon pathways.

bullets: Scientific paper (2026): first author on urban energy models and renovation strategies in the context of climate action plans (PCAET).
ADEME report: modelling and analysis for low-carbon trajectories (MAP 2050 project, CEEP MINES ParisTech).
SmartE refactoring: transformed 10 years of technical debt into a modular Python library (53,000+ lines).
Designed and taught a course for around 15 engineering students.
Content: data processing, web scraping, OOP, best practices, Git/GitHub.
Supported 12 clients on collective self-consumption projects.
Developed a Python module for project sizing (50+ participants), integrated into Enolab.

experiences.org: Buildings Department, City Hall of Saint-Denis
bullets: Site supervision for health and early childhood facilities.
Managed and trained users on fablab equipment.

projets.desc: Python tool for multi-format data reading (DataFrame, CSV, GeoJSON, GeoPackage). github.com/CES-MDE/dataToolBox
Python calculation library for sizing self-consumption projects (load curves, PV, financial).
Fully editable online CV: inline editing, multi‑profile (localStorage/JSON), light/dark theme, accent color picker, drag & drop, PDF auto‑scaling.

interets: - Krav Maga – 4 years - President, ACE (Energy Design Area) 2022–2023 - Treasurer, T4EG (Together For Earth Grenoble) 2022–2023

etudes.degree: Master of Engineering — Energy Systems and Markets
Preparatory Classes PSI* (Physics and Engineering Sciences)
